channel.title: Articles on Bali Kini
channel.description: Recent content in Articles on Bali Kini

item.title: Balinale 2026: 94 Films from 38 Countries Illuminate Sanur
item.description: Sanur, Bali has become the center of the international film world’s attention. The Bali International Film Festival, widely known as Balinale, is celebrating its 19th edition from June 1–7, 2026. Ninety-four films from 38 countries are being screened across multiple venues in the Sanur area, cementing Balinale’s position as one of Southeast Asia’s most prestigious film festivals. Theme and Spirit of the 19th Edition Carrying the theme “Stories Worth Experiencing”, Balinale 2026 arrives with a spirit of celebrating the power of cinema as a universal storytelling medium.

item.title: Bali Tourism June 2026: 553K Visitors in April, Target 6.6M for the Year
item.description: Bali welcomed 553,000 foreign visitors in April 2026, according to the latest data from BPS Bali (Statistics Indonesia). While this represents a 6.41% decline year-on-year, the island’s tourism economy tells a more nuanced story. The Visitor Paradox Despite fewer international arrivals, hotel occupancy rates across Bali actually increased during the same period. Analysts attribute this anomaly to a surge in domestic tourism — Indonesian travelers from Java and other islands filling the rooms that fewer foreign visitors left empty.

item.title: PORJAR Bali 2026: Thousands of Student Athletes Compete in 39 Sports
item.description: Bali’s Pekan Olahraga Pelajar (PORJAR) — the Student Sports Week — officially begins on June 5–10, 2026 across Denpasar and multiple venues throughout the Island of the Gods. This prestigious biennial multi-sport event once again serves as a grand stage for thousands of Bali’s finest young athletes to compete for glory at the provincial level. What Is PORJAR Bali? PORJAR is a provincial-level multi-sport competition exclusively for elementary, middle, and high school students across Bali.

item.title: 7 Best Sunset Spots in Bali You Must Visit in 2026
item.description: Bali’s late afternoons possess a particular kind of magic. As the sun tilts westward, the sky transforms into a vast canvas of orange, pink and violet — a spectacle photographed a million times, yet never losing its power. If you’re in Bali and wondering where to spend your evening, here are the seven sunset spots that consistently deliver. 1. Tanah Lot — The Temple on the Sea Nothing rivals Tanah Lot at golden hour.

item.title: Bali News Recap: High Waves, Clear Skies & Sacred Calendar — June 3, 2026
item.description: Wednesday, June 3, 2026. Here is today’s summary of the key news, alerts, and information from around Bali — from natural conditions and safety warnings to the sacred calendar you need to know this month. ⚠️ High Wave Warning: June 1–4 BMKG (Indonesia’s Meteorology, Climatology and Geophysics Agency) has issued a warning for high waves of 2.5–4 metres in the Badung Strait and South Bali waters. Coastal flooding risk applies to areas along Jembrana, Tabanan, Badung, Denpasar, Gianyar, and Klungkung.

item.title: Galungan 2026: The Complete Guide for Visitors and Locals
item.description: Galungan 2026 falls on June 17, 2026, followed by Kuningan on June 27, 2026. For the Balinese, this is the most sacred moment in the Hindu Balinese calendar — and for visitors, it is simply the best time to see Bali in its most authentic form. What Is Galungan? Galungan celebrates the victory of dharma (good) over adharma (evil). The festival occurs every 210 days according to the Balinese Pawukon calendar.

item.title: Subak: How Water Teaches Humanity About Unity and the Sacred
item.description: On a quiet morning in the highlands of Tabanan, an elderly farmer walks along the narrow dykes of his rice field. He is not simply checking his crop — he is fulfilling a duty to his community, to nature, and to God. The system that governs his life has existed for more than a thousand years. It is called Subak. More Than Just an Irrigation System Subak is often simplified as “Bali’s traditional irrigation system.
